title: Problem Framing Check
description: Use AI follow-up questions to check whether you can apply the goal-to-problem distinction to your own work.

questions.prompt: Explain the difference between your starting goal, any surface request you noticed, and the underlying problem you are now trying to frame.
questions.placeholder: Name the starting goal, the surface request if there is one, and the specific condition that makes the goal hard to reach.
questions.aiContext: This is a low-stakes Sprint 1 concept check. The participant should show that a goal names a desired direction while a problem names the condition making that direction hard to reach. Push them to avoid solution-first phrasing, broad aspirations, and unsupported claims. Ask for one kind of evidence that could make the first problem frame stronger.
questions.discussionPrompt: Use the follow-up questions to revise your answer. Your refined response should show the distinction between goal, surface request, underlying problem, and evidence.
questions.summaryPlaceholder: Rewrite your answer so an outside reader can see the goal, the underlying problem, and one piece of evidence that would strengthen the frame.
